Title: Lima 2027 promises biggest defence showcase, 400,000 visitors expected
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 18th Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition (Lima 2027) is targeting 400,000 trade and public visitors, a 15 per cent increase from previous editions.
Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said visitors will get a first-hand look at the Malaysian Armed Forces' latest acquisitions, including the FA-50 Light Combat Aircraft, the ANKA unmanned aerial vehicle from Türkiye and the Littoral Mission Ship (LMS) Batch 2.
Malek, while delivering the speech of Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Khaled Nordin at the launch event today, said Lima 2027 aims to strengthen Malaysia's position as a strategic defence, maritime and aerospace hub in the Asia-Pacific region.
This, he said, aligned with the National Defence Policy, the National Defence Industry Policy and the National Defence Strategic Plan.
He said the event is expected to attract about 930 exhibitors from 35 countries, more than 450 official delegations representing 65 nations, as well as 110 aircraft and 90 maritime assets.
He added that exhibitor response had been encouraging, with about 40 per cent of exhibition space booked within two months of sales opening.
"It encompasses every aspect of operational readiness of the armed forces, the strategic direction with the ministry, and efforts to strengthen the national defence industry," Malek said.
"Within two months, about 40 per cent of the exhibition space has already been reserved by exhibitors from Malaysia and abroad," he added.
In an accompanying statement, the Defence Ministry said Lima 2027 would place greater emphasis on next-generation defence technologies, including autonomous maritime systems, unmanned aerial vehicles, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, satellite communications, space technology, advanced weapons systems, and humanitarian assistance and disaster relief capabilities.
It said the exhibition would also strengthen defence diplomacy, expand defence industry supply chains and create new opportunities for Malaysian companies to penetrate international markets.
Under the theme 'Sustainable Vision Across Sea, Sky and Space', Lima 2027 will be held in Langkawi, Kedah, from April 20 to 24 next year.
It is set to feature four of the world's premier aerobatic display teams — South Korea's Black Eagles, the United Arab Emirates' Al Fursan Display Team, Indonesia's Jupiter Aerobatic Team and Russia's Russian Knights.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Alliance Bank expands financial literacy drive for students
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Alliance Bank Malaysia Bhd has expanded its financial literacy programme for students, including secondary schools and introduced biodiversity education.
The bank's eighth edition of the AEIOU Seeds for Life programme will also be extended to schools in the east coast as well as selected international and private schools for the first time.
Supported by the  Education Ministry, the programme reflects a shared commitment between the public and private sectors to equip the next generation with the knowledge, values and practical skills needed for the future.
Deputy Education Minister Wong Kah Woh said the introduction of a biodiversity module comes as climate change continues to pose growing challenges and complements the ministry's new Alam dan Manusia subject.
Alliance Bank group chief strategy and transformation officer Dr Aaron Sum said the module teaches students how actions such as conserving water, using energy efficiently and choosing environmentally friendly products can reduce costs while benefiting the environment.
"This highlights how sound financial management and environmental sustainability are closely linked," he said.
To support the biodiversity component, Alliance Bank has partnered with environmental non-governmental organisation Jane Goodall's Roots & Shoots Malaysia to develop curriculum content and conduct workshops.
The programme includes two national competitions: the AEIOU Eco-Comic Challenge for primary school students and the AEIOU Eco-Pitching Challenge for secondary school students, offering a total prize pool of RM35,000.
Since its launch in 2015, the programme has reached more than 148,000 students through roadshows and learning activities.
It has also trained about 2,900 teachers and involved more than 3,510 schools nationwide.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DOE denies publishing viral cleanliness ranking infographic
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Department of Environment (DOE) has denied issuing, publishing or verifying the infographic titled "Ranking Kebersihan Negeri Malaysia 2024", which has been widely circulated on social media platforms and messaging applications.
The DOE said in a statement today that it had never released any media statement, report or response regarding the purported ranking of states as displayed in the infographic.
"The public is advised not to disseminate the information or use it as an official reference without first verifying it with the department.
"Members of the public are also advised not to believe, disseminate or share unverified information, as it may mislead others and create misunderstandings about the actual facts," the statement said.
The department said the spread of unverified information could not only mislead the public but also potentially erode public confidence in official information relating to environmental management and conservation.
To avoid confusion, the DOE said all official media statements, reports, statistics, infographics, announcements and information issued by the department would only be published through its official communication channels, namely its official portal at www.doe.gov.my, Facebook (@JabatanAlamSekitar), Instagram (@jabatanalamsekitar), X (@jasmalaysia), TikTok (@jas_malaysia_official) and YouTube (@JabatanAlamSekitar).
The department stressed that it took seriously any misuse of its name, logo or corporate identity, as well as the dissemination of misleading information that could affect its reputation and credibility.
"In this regard, the DOE will not hesitate to take appropriate action in accordance with the provisions of the law against any party found to have misused the department's identity or spread false information by linking it to the department.
"The DOE remains committed to ensuring that all information conveyed to the public is accurate, authentic, transparent and credible, in line with its responsibilities as the authority responsible for the management and protection of the country's environment," the statement said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia's navy faces 2-year wait for new missile system
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Replacing the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system could take at least two years to complete, with the Armed Forces working to address a capability gap affecting the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), says Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman.
He said the delay was a setback as commissioned navy ships currently lacked missile capability.
"The ships are ready and have been commissioned, but we lack the weapons or ammunition.
"That is a setback, so we have to facilitate and mitigate it," he added.
Malek said the timeline would depend on the procurement process and available budget.
"It is a new process to look at, and it depends on the budget. As I said, it is a setback to our readiness for now," he told reporters after launching the 18th Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition (Lima 2027) today.
He said France, Turkiye, Italy and South Korea had been shortlisted as potential suppliers for the replacement system following operational and technical evaluations by the RMN and the Defence Ministry.
"Those are the four countries shortlisted based on operational and technical evaluations by the Navy and the ministry," he said.
However, Malek said no final decision had been made as the procurement tender process had yet to begin.
Yesterday, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said Malaysia would begin its
first round
of negotiations with Norway's Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace (KDA) in mid-August to seek an amicable settlement over the cancellation of the NSM export licence.
He said that Malaysia could face a lengthy legal process if the negotiations failed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: E-waste container misdeclared as tin ore catches fire at Port Klang
Article:
PORT KLANG: A shipping container falsely declared as carrying tin ore caught fire shortly after arriving at Port Klang, where inspections later revealed it was filled with electronic waste (e-waste).
The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) said the incident occurred at 7.05am yesterday while the container was on a trailer after reaching the port.
Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the container arrived from overseas on July 6 with a declared cargo weight of 17,237kg.
He said the shipping documents, including the bill of lading, listed the cargo as tin ore.
"Following the incident, a joint inspection was carried out by the MCBA and the Department of Environment.
"The inspection found the container was filled entirely with electrical and electronic waste, not tin ore as declared in the shipping documents," he said.
Nik Ezanee said the Customs Department was investigating possible offences, including false declaration, importing controlled goods without complying with the required conditions, and other related breaches.
He said stern action would be taken against those found to have violated import regulations, adding that compliance was essential to safeguard public safety, the environment and national interests.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zambry: New higher education programmes in Sabah will target state's economic needs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Higher Education Ministry is open to applications for new academic programmes in Sabah to broaden study options at public higher education institutions (IPTA) in the state.
Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said applications for programmes at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), polytechnics, and community colleges would be assessed based on institutional expertise, industry needs, student demand, and graduate employability.
"This approach ensures the programmes offered are of quality, relevant, and aligned with Sabah's economic development needs, while optimising existing expertise and resources and avoiding duplication among IPTAs," he said during Ministers' Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to Mohd Kurniawan Naim Moktar (BN-Kinabatangan) on measures to increase the capacity and diversity of IPTA programmes in Sabah to reduce students' reliance on campuses in Peninsular Malaysia.
Zambry said the ministry is committed to expanding higher education opportunities in the state.
"Until June 30, a total of 16 IPTAs, including branch campuses, were operating in Sabah. These comprise four public universities, three polytechnics, and nine community colleges," he said.
He said UMS and its branch campuses are developing programmes based on research strengths and Borneo's unique biodiversity, including marine science, aquaculture, tropical biotechnology, medical sciences, heritage, ecotourism, and business.
Meanwhile, the Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Sabah branch focuses on tourism, hospitality, business, administrative sciences, and science and technology.
Zambry said the ministry is implementing 21 development projects in Sabah costing RM1.05 billion to support higher education capacity, with RM160.6 million allocated under the First Rolling Plan of the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) for 2026.
He added that the ministry does not set specific quotas for higher education placements, as admission depends on course availability and demand.
"We cannot simply set a target, such as 80 per cent of programmes being offered in Sabah, as some courses are also available elsewhere, particularly in Peninsular Malaysia and Sarawak.
"However, we continue to plan for better access through a niche-based approach, such as law programmes, as Sabah and Sarawak have a strong need for these fields," he said.
Responding to a supplementary question from Dr Halimah Ali (PN-Kapar), Zambry said the government provides funding to support university research activities, including projects under the Research, Development, Innovation, Commercialisation and Economy (RDICE) programme, which focuses on commercial value.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia ranked world's 6th-best medical tourism destination, led by Penang
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Malaysia has been ranked the world's sixth-best medical tourism destination, with Penang continuing to lead the country's healthcare travel sector.
State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai described the achievement as a proud moment for both the nation and the state.
"Congratulations, Malaysia, and congratulations, Penang!
"The latest ranking of the world's Top 10 Medical Tourism Destinations is a proud achievement that reflects the collective efforts of Malaysia's healthcare system, tourism industry and government agencies over many years.
"Malaysia has emerged as one of Asia's leading medical tourism destinations, supported by internationally accredited hospitals, English-speaking healthcare professionals, modern medical technologies, affordable healthcare services and strong government support," he said in a Facebook post.
(https://www.facebook.com/share/1BVY5P1kXG/?mibextid=wwXIfr)
According to the 2026 Global Top 10 Medical Tourism Destinations ranking published by Travel And Tour World, Turkey topped the list, followed by Thailand, India, Mexico, South Korea, Malaysia, Costa Rica, Singapore, the United Arab Emirates and Colombia.
The report said Turkey secured first place due to its affordable treatment costs, advanced medical technologies and high-quality healthcare services, while Malaysia's ranking was driven by its internationally accredited hospitals, English-speaking medical workforce and well-developed medical tourism ecosystem.
It also noted that Malaysia is particularly sought after for cardiology, oncology, fertility treatments, orthopaedics, cosmetic and plastic surgery, dental care, ophthalmology, health screening, rehabilitation services and wellness programmes.
According to the latest figures, Malaysia's medical tourism revenue increased 23.2 per cent to RM3.35 billion in 2025 from RM2.72 billion in 2024.
The number of medical tourists also grew 15.6 per cent to 1.85 million in 2025 from 1.6 million a year earlier, reflecting continued international confidence in the country's healthcare services and competitive pricing.
Wong said Penang remained Malaysia's leading medical tourism destination, contributing about 45 per cent of the country's total medical tourism revenue.
Data from the Penang Centre of Medical Tourism (PMED), covering 16 private hospitals in the state, showed Penang received 527,176 foreign patients in 2025, up 25.94 per cent from 418,608 in 2024.
Medical tourism revenue in the state rose 26.6 per cent to RM1.137 billion in 2025 from RM898.07 million the previous year.
"Penang's sustained leadership is driven by several key advantages, particularly its ability to provide exceptional value for money through high-quality healthcare services at competitive prices.
"Compared with Singapore and many Western countries, patients can enjoy substantial cost savings while receiving internationally recognised standards of care.
"We also have a significant multilingual advantage. Penang's population is proficient in Malay, English, Mandarin, Tamil and Chinese dialects including Hokkien, Teochew, Hakka and Cantonese, enabling effective communication with international patients.
"For Indonesian patients, especially those from Medan who commonly speak Hokkien, language barriers are minimal.
This enhances their overall experience and further strengthens Penang's position as the preferred medical tourism destination for Indonesian patients," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz: BN won't be complacent despite poll forecast
Article:
PONTIAN: Barisan Nasional (BN) will not be complacent despite a survey projecting a comfortable victory in Saturday's state election, says Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.
Responding to a Merdeka Centre forecast that BN could secure between 40 and 42 of the 56 state seats, he said the findings were merely a reference, with the coalition determined to campaign until the final hour.
"There are various surveys and analyses, but whatever the findings, the BN machinery in Johor will continue working as hard as possible until the last day.
"We will leave no stone unturned. We will not take anything for granted," he said after meeting residents in Pekan Nanas today.
With four days of campaigning remaining before polling on July 11, Onn Hafiz said BN's priority was to reach as many voters as possible across the state.
He also urged voters to back BN's Pekan Nanas candidate Tan Eng Meng, describing him as a hardworking leader who had consistently served the constituency since the last election.
"For the past four years, who has been doing the work in Pekan Nanas? Did anyone see the previous losing candidate around? Did that person help the community here? Now they have only reappeared because there is an election," he said.
Tan is seeking to retain the Pekan Nanas seat after winning it with a 4,835-vote majority in a four-cornered contest in the 2022 Johor election.
In the 2022 state election, BN won 40 of the 56 seats to secure a two-thirds majority, while Pakatan Harapan took 12 seats, Perikatan Nasional three and Muda one.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara international reserves rise to US$132.6bil at end-June
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia's international reserves rose to US$132.6 billion as at June 30, 2026, slightly more than the US$130.5 billion recorded as at June 15.
In a statement today, the central bank said the reserves level had taken into account the quarterly foreign exchange revaluation changes.
Bank Negara said the reserves position was sufficient to finance 4.7 months of imports of goods and services and was equivalent to 0.9 times the country's total short-term external debt.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Government studies Child Act reforms over indefinite detention of children – Kulasegaran
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Forty people remain detained under the Child Act 2001, with some held for up to 25 years, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.
Deputy Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Law and Institutional Reform) M. Kulasegaran said the detainees comprised four individuals held for less than a year, 14 for between one and five years, seven for five to 10 years, eight for 10 to 15 years, four for 15 to 20 years, and three for 20 to 25 years.
He said the government is studying amendments to the Act to align it with the best interests of children and international standards.
"The government takes seriously the issue of children being detained under the Act, particularly regarding the absence of a prescribed detention period under the existing provision.
"This provision also needs to be reviewed following the abolition of mandatory death sentences and life imprisonment through the Abolition of Mandatory Death Penalty Act 2023," he said.
Kulasegaran was responding to S. Kesavan (PH–Sungai Siput) regarding juvenile detention figures and ongoing legal reform efforts.
He said a special committee to review and amend the Child Act 2001 had been established, comprising representatives from ministries and government agencies, legal professional bodies and stakeholders, including the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia and Unicef.
"Currently, the government is in the phase of reviewing and discussing policy proposals on the amendments, including setting clearer detention periods, establishing annual review mechanisms and strengthening rehabilitation and reintegration approaches for children into society," he said.
Kulasegaran said the government was committed to ensuring any proposed amendments strike a balance between justice, public safety and child welfare, in line with Malaysia's human rights commitments.
He added that comparative legal studies had been conducted involving about 25 countries, including the United Kingdom, Australia, Qatar and India.
"The study found that most countries set maximum detention periods for children to prevent prolonged detention.
"In Indonesia and Bangladesh, for offences punishable by death or life imprisonment, children can only be sentenced to a maximum of 10 years' imprisonment," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MASkargo, Qatar Airways Cargo launch KL-Bengaluru-Doha freighter link
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: MAB Kargo Sdn Bhd (MASkargo) has expanded its partnership with Qatar Airways Cargo and launched a twice-weekly freighter service linking Kuala Lumpur, Bengaluru and Doha.
The service will be operated using Qatar Airways' Boeing 777 freighters, adding 200 tonnes of weekly cargo capacity across the network for shipments including general cargo, perishables, pharmaceuticals and valuables.
MASkargo, the logistics arm of Malaysia Aviation Group, said the partnership combines its extensive Asian network with Qatar Airways Cargo's global reach.
This enhances connectivity to key markets across Asia, Europe and North America, with Kuala Lumpur and Bengaluru serving as dedicated gateways.
"Since the launch of the twice-weekly freighter operations, more than 1,000 tonnes of cargo have been transported, underscoring strong customer demand for enhanced cargo connectivity.
"The partnership continues to strengthen network resilience, support Asia-linked trade flows and broaden access to key international markets in an increasingly dynamic global trade environment," it said.
Its chief executive officer Mark Jason Thomas said the collaboration underscores the company's commitment to reinforcing Kuala Lumpur's role as a strategic cargo gateway linking Asia with major global trade routes.
"In line with our broader growth ambitions, this collaboration supports our focus on optimising the cargo network through enhanced connectivity, greater operational flexibility and more agile logistics solutions that meet the evolving demands of global trade and high-value freight movements," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RNG Tech targets 1,570 new massage chairs to drive regional expansion
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RNG Tech Bhd, the operator of the Rest N Go vending massage chair network, plans to deploy about 1,570 vending massage chairs across Malaysia and overseas markets as it enters its next phase of regional growth.
The company said more than half of the planned expansion will take place this year. The rollout will comprise 360 chairs in Malaysia and 450 units across overseas markets, including Singapore (100 units), Thailand (200 units), Cambodia (100 units) and Brunei (50 units).
Executive director and group chief financial officer Shin Kok Leong said the domestic expansion will focus on high-traffic locations such as shopping malls, transport hubs and airports, although specific sites have yet to be finalised.
"We do not have specific locations yet, but we will focus on high-traffic areas such as shopping malls, transport hubs and airports," he told reporters after the company's listing ceremony today.
As of May 18, RNG Tech owned and operated 5,611 vending massage chairs across Malaysia, Singapore, Thailand, Cambodia and Brunei.
The company debuted flat on the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia on Tuesday, opening unchanged at its initial public offering price of 13 sen.
The stock hit an intraday high of 13.5 sen in the morning trading session and dropped to as low as 12.5 sen.
At the midday break, RNG's share price stood unchanged at 13 sen with more than 80 million shares traded.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: 588 election offences reported as of today, says EC
Article:
KLUANG: A total of 588 election offences have been recorded throughout the Johor election campaign as of today.
Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said 44 police reports had been lodged, while three reports had been submitted to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC).
Ramlan also said the commission was maintaining its projection of a 96 per cent turnout for early voting today.
"Based on our previous achievements, early voting turnout can reach up to 96 per cent and we are maintaining that projection," he told reporters after inspecting the early voting process at Kem Mahkota Kluang today.
Also present was Army chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman.
Ramlan said early voter turnout stood at 82 per cent as of 1pm today.
"Sixty-four early voting centres were opened across Johor about 8am today.
"Several categories of centres are involved, with 29 early voting centres having closed at noon, while another five closed at 2pm.
"The remaining 28 early voting centres will close at 5pm.
"Insya-Allah, the early votes will be counted on the actual polling day on July 11, with the counting process expected to begin at around 5pm," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: BN Youth raises concerns over government agencies' role in election dialogue
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) Youth has questioned the involvement of government agencies and civil servants in a state election dialogue scheduled at the RTM Johor Auditorium tonight.
Its chief, Noor Azleen Ambros, raised concerns over the neutrality of public institutions during the campaign period after a notice issued by the Johor Broadcasting Department listed about 200 participants.
This includes civil servants and representatives from agencies such as the Johor National Security Council, Community Communications Department (J-KOM) and the Prime Minister's Department Implementation Coordination Unit in Johor.
Azleen said voters had the right to know whether government institutions were maintaining a clear separation between official functions and political activities.
He questioned the participation of government agencies, the use of a government facility as the venue, and the measures in place to ensure the programme remained impartial.
"If the objective is to provide equal space to all contesting parties, why were representatives from Perikatan Nasional (PN), BERSAMA, Muda and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) not invited?" he said in a statement today.
The programme, titled Dialog Pilihan Raya Negeri (PRN) Johor, is expected to be attended by Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil and Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for Puteri Wangsa, Dr Maszlee Malik.
Noor Azleen stressed that his remarks were not an accusation, but a call for accountability and transparency from public institutions.
He said government bodies must not only remain neutral but also be seen as neutral, as public confidence depends on both actions and perception.
"Government institutions belong to all Malaysians, not to whichever administration is in power at a particular time," he said.
He added that during an election period, public institutions must avoid any perception of favouring any political party or coalition to uphold the integrity of the democratic process.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: International students offer much in a borderless world
Article:
LETTERS:
Malaysia has increasingly established itself as a preferred destination for foreign students pursuing higher education, reflecting the international community's confidence in the quality of the country's education system.
Nevertheless, there remains a perception that the presence of international students reduces opportunities for Malaysian students to gain admission to higher education institutions.
In reality, local and international students are admitted through different admission pathways and are subject to distinct policies and requirements established by the relevant authorities and institutions.
Consequently, the presence of international students does not diminish local students' access to higher education. Rather, Malaysia's internationalisation agenda is designed to strengthen educational quality, expand academic networks, and expose students to a globally oriented learning environment.
More importantly, international students enrich the overall learning experience on campus.
In most academic programmes, they represent only a small proportion of the total student population, with Malaysian students continuing to make up the overwhelming majority.
This diversity creates a more dynamic learning environment and provides Malaysian students with valuable opportunities to engage with peers from different countries and cultural backgrounds.
As a university lecturer who teaches classes attended by both Malaysian and international students, I have witnessed firsthand the benefits of this diversity.
In every class, international students are encouraged not to remain within their own social circles but instead to interact, collaborate, and complete group assignments alongside Malaysian students. Likewise, Malaysian students are encouraged to embrace their international peers as members of the same learning community.
Such interactions not only facilitate the exchange of knowledge but also help break down cultural barriers that might otherwise exist when students interact only within their own groups.
This approach fosters a more inclusive learning culture. Through classroom discussions and collaborative assignments, students learn to respect differing perspectives, appreciate cultural diversity, and develop essential communication and teamwork skills.
Malaysian students also become more confident in using English in authentic situations, thereby improving their self-confidence and ability to communicate in international settings. They are exposed to new ways of thinking and problem-solving, as each student brings unique experiences, cultural values, and perspectives to the learning process.
International students gain a deeper understanding of Malaysian culture, values, and society beyond the classroom through co-curricular activities, volunteer programmes, and campus life.
In today's workplace, employers seek graduates who are not only academically competent but also capable of working effectively with people from diverse cultural backgrounds. Cross-cultural communication, empathy, leadership, adaptability, and teamwork have become highly valued attributes that distinguish graduates in an increasingly competitive global job market.
The internationalisation of higher education is an opportunity to build more inclusive campuses and produce graduates prepared to thrive in a borderless world.
DR HIDAYATULLAH MOHAMMAD ALI
Senior Lecturer,
Department of Aerospace Engineering,
Faculty of Engineering,
Universiti Putra Malaysia
The views expressed in this article are the author's own and do not necessarily reflect those of the New Straits Times
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: VAT 69: Five decades of defending Malaysia from insurgents, terrorists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The 69 Commando, popularly known as Very Able Troopers 69 (VAT 69), is Malaysia's premier special operations unit, operating under the police Special Operations Command (PGK).
Established in 1969 at the height of the Second Malayan Emergency, the elite force was created to combat the resurgence of the communist insurgency that erupted in 1968, nearly a decade after the end of the First Malayan Emergency.
Its primary mission was to conduct deep jungle operations against communist guerrillas who originally fought for British colonial liberation, including fighters aligned with the Malayan Communist Party (MCP) and sympathisers of the movement re-emboldened by the Cold War.
The unit was modelled closely after the British 22nd Special Air Service (22 SAS) Regiment and the New Zealand SAS, whose advisers played a significant role in shaping its early training and doctrine.
VAT 69 saw extensive action during the insurgency, conducting long-range patrols deep into the Malaysia-Thai border region where resistance fighters maintained their positions.
Following the formal end of the insurgency after the 1989 Hat Yai Peace Agreement with the MCP, the unit shifted its focus toward counter-terrorism and organised crime.
VAT 69's operational record spans more than five decades of critical national service, from the Al-Maunah armed standoff in 2000, the Lahad Datu incursion in 2013 under Ops Daulat, and special national assignments connected to the MH17 tragedy in 2014.
Earlier today, the Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Muizzuddin Shah, officially opened the
Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp
, marking a major milestone in strengthening the country's elite counter-terrorism force.
In his speech, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the opening of the camp represents more than the completion of a new facility, as it reflects the government's commitment to strengthening the nation's security capabilities amid increasingly complex and evolving threats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korean stocks tumble as AI earnings boom faces doubts
Article:
SINGAPORE: South Korean equities dropped eight per cent on Tuesday, triggering a circuit breaker, dragged lower by a steep sell-off in Samsung Electronics shares amid investor concerns about the durability of the AI-driven earnings boom.
The MSCI EM Asia index fell nearly three per cent, marking its weakest session in nearly two weeks, as chip-heavy KOSPI index tumbled eight per cent to approach a one-month low.
Taiwan's benchmark gauge also slipped more than two per cent. Samsung Electronics tanked 10 per cent as investors looked beyond the South Korean electronics giant's forecast of a 19-fold jump in second-quarter earnings, with results failing to alleviate concerns about the sustainability of the AI-driven chip boom.
"Samsung's decline suggests the AI trade is becoming increasingly valuation-sensitive," said Glenn Yin, director of research at ACCM.
The market needs evidence that AI spending and earnings growth can keep accelerating, otherwise, tech-heavy Asian markets, where performance has grown increasingly concentrated in a handful of AI-related companies, could face a rapid de-rating, Yin added.
In Southeast Asia, Thai stocks fell as much as 1.1 per cent, as electronics manufacturer Delta Electronics Thailand declined nearly three per cent to become the top laggard on the benchmark. Singaporean stocks rose about one per cent to a record high, as shares of OCBC, UOB and DBS gained one per cent to two per cent.
Stocks in Jakarta advanced 0.5 per cent to a more than one week high, while the rupiah lingered around 17,980 a dollar. In the Philippines, shares rose 0.7 per cent after data showed annual inflation slowed in June.
The central bank also said it was prepared to take further monetary action as needed. The next scheduled policy review is on August 27. "While the June CPI print offers some reassurance that inflation has peaked, underlying price pressures remain elevated.
This is likely to keep the central bank cautious for several reasons," said Deepali Bhargava, ING's Asia-Pacific regional head of research. In Malaysia, stocks edged lower while the ringgit appreciated to 4.0720 a dollar.
The currency has firmed by around 1.5 per cent since June 24 when the central bank announced measures to support it after it had weakened more than five per cent earlier that month.
Bank Negara Malaysia is expected to keep its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a sixth straight meeting at 2.75 per cent on Thursday, a Reuters poll showed, as inflation is subdued and economic growth continues to be strong.
Elsewhere, the South Korean won appreciated 0.5 per cent to 1,519 a dollar for the first time in nearly three weeks, while the Philippine peso was largely unchanged.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: HSBC reins in riskier private credit lending
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: HSBC is pulling back from riskier private credit lending, becoming the latest bank to rein in exposure to the sector after a string of high-profile bankruptcies raised concerns over underwriting standards, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday.
The bank has told some clients it will not renew their lending facilities after deciding to stop lending to private credit funds that did not offer sufficient returns to justify the risk, the report, citing three people familiar with the matter, said. It will instead focus on lower-risk private credit funds, the report added.
"We have built an offering that covers every stage of the private credit market, offering a seamless process with robust central oversight. We focus on supporting deals globally for our most important clients, in regions where we see the most potential for growth and aligned to our strategy," an HSBC spokesperson told Reuters in an emailed statement.
Exposure to private credit lending has troubled Europe's biggest lender and added to the market jitters around the sector.
HSBC in May took a US$400 million hit linked to ⁠the collapse of British mortgage lender Market Financial Solutions.
Reports later that month said that HSBC had paused a planned US$4 billion investment in its own private credit funds, and the bank affirmed at the time that it remains committed to its private credit investments.
The FT report underscores the growing scrutiny of private credit portfolios, with regulators worldwide becoming ​increasingly concerned about banks' exposure to the US$3.5 trillion private credit industry.
Wealthy investors have queued up to withdraw their money from private credit vehicles in recent months amid worries about weakening lending standards and fears of AI-driven disruption at software companies that have borrowed from direct lenders.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Former BH journalist Jailani Harun dies at 66
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Former Berita Harian journalist Datuk Jailani Harun, who served as a diplomatic officer to Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, died at the Serdang Hospital today. He was 66.
The news of Jailani's death at 11.34am was shared by the Deputy Prime Minister's office and confirmed by his eldest daughter, Nabilla, 33.
His body will be taken to Al-Mubarakah Mosque in Taman Bukit Cheras for funeral prayers before being laid to rest at the Alam Damai Cheras Cemetery after Asar prayers.
Meanwhile, Communications Minister Fahmi Fadzil conveyed his condolences to Jailani's family and friends.
"May they be granted strength and patience in facing this loss. Let us pray that Allah SWT showers Jailani's soul with mercy and places him among the righteous," he said in a Facebook post.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lynas, South Korea firm sign deal for magnet factory in Malaysia [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Australian rare earths company Lynas said Tuesday it has partnered with South Korea's JS Link to build a permanent magnet factory in eastern Malaysia, a move that links its raw material production with downstream manufacturing.
Lynas, which operates the largest commercial rare earths processing plant outside China, said it will invest about US$35 million in the facility near its site in Kuantan, around 250 kilometres (160 miles) east of Kuala Lumpur.
The plant is expected to produce 3,000 tonnes of magnets annually using the rare earth element neodymium, with iron and boron.
The magnets are "intended to supply automotive, wind energy and electronics manufacturing supply chains including in  South Korea, Malaysia "and other key markets", Lynas said in a statement.
Under the agreement, Lynas will supply rare earth materials to the new Malaysian plant as well as JS Link's facility in Yesan, South Korea, under an exclusive commercial arrangement running until January 2038.
The project is expected to create around 400 jobs.
Lynas's Gebeng facility, which has been running since 2012, supplies materials used in a range of products including electric vehicles, mobile phones and missiles.
It has cornered around 10 percent of the world's rare-earth market, with the other 90 percent made in China.
Malaysia has around 274,144 metric tonnes of rare earth minerals, Malaysia's Environmental Sustainability Minister said in January.
Other rare earths firms are also eyeing Malaysia as a strategic focal point for the sought-after resource.
French company Carester on Monday announced plans with local firm Malaco Mining to build a rare earths separation plant in the northern Perak state under a 10-year joint venture.
The facility is expected to process around 13,000 tonnes annually, Carester chief executive Frederic Carencotte said.
But rare earth production has also come under fire in Malaysia, particularly over environmental concerns and the supply to the arms manufacturing industry.
Also on Monday, a group of 20 NGOs protested outside the Malaysian parliament, where they handed over a memorandum calling for stronger oversight of the supply chain of rare earths leaving the country.
"Malaysia has an opportunity to demonstrate leadership by strengthening transparency, accountability, and human rights safeguards across the rare earth sector," the group said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: PKR's Shamsul under fire over talkin-inspired video
Article:
JOHOR BARU: An election campaign video by PKR leader Datuk Seri Shamsul Iskandar Mohd Akin, featuring elements resembling the Islamic talkin recitation, has sparked criticism online.
The use of the talkin concept has drawn criticism on social media, with several users questioning whether a religious practice should be used as a political campaign device.
The backlash intensified after he appeared to encourage voters to accept cash and gifts from rival parties, a practice condemned in Islam.
Some netizens further criticised him for allegedly using religion as a form of mockery, while others commented that such remarks were expected from "someone who is used to taking bribes".
Shamsul, who was the Prime Minister's former political secretary, has pleaded not guilty to receiving RM176,829.03 in alleged graft from businessman Albert Tei.
The PKR Melaka chief
entered his plea
after he was charged with four counts of corruptly accepting cash and household items from Tei as inducements to assist companies linked to the businessman in securing mineral exploration licences in Sabah.
In the video, Shamsul appeared dressed in a jubah and kopiah against a sombre backdrop, using an echo effect to mimic the solemn tone associated with talkin.
Addressing Johor voters, Shamsul said they should not fear approaches from rival parties, describing leaders from Barisan Nasional (BN), Perikatan Nasional (PN) and BERSAMA as fellow creations of God.
He also urged voters not to reject any offerings made during the campaign, but to maintain their political choice.
"When you are given RM100, ask for RM500. When you are given coffee and tea, ask for Milo, milk and enough rice for a month," he said in the video.
"However, when they ask you to vote for them, answer clearly that we will still vote for Pakatan Harapan."
In Islamic tradition, talkin is a recitation delivered at the graveside as a reminder for the deceased to affirm their faith when questioned about their religious beliefs. It also serves as a reminder for the living to remain committed to their obligations.
Meanwhile, Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambrose criticised the video, saying Shamsul should be mindful of the sensitivities surrounding religious matters.
He also took a swipe at PH's political standing, referring to past election defeats.
"Do not play with talkin. Be careful, or PH may be the one receiving the talkin in Johor this Saturday," he told the New Straits Times.
The controversy comes as parties intensify their final campaign push ahead of polling day on July 11, with candidates racing to secure support from voters in the closing stretch.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 71,101 holiday homes generated RM2.2bil revenue in 2025: DOSM
Article:
PUTRAJAYA:  A total of 71,101 holiday homes were operating nationwide in 2025, generating an estimated RM2.2 billion in revenue, according to the Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 published by the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) today.
Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the industry's continued growth reflected increasing demand for alternative accommodation and its growing contribution to tourism-related activities and the services sector.
"The holiday homes industry continues to create positive economic spillover effects through higher demand for accommodation, transportation, food and beverage services, retail trade and recreational activities.
"It also provides income opportunities for local communities and micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), while supporting more balanced regional development and sustainable tourism growth," he said in a statement.
Mohd Uzir said Selangor recorded the highest number of holiday homes at 19,095 units, representing more than a quarter of the national total, followed by Kuala Lumpur with 8,207 units and Johor with 7,274 units.
He said the concentration of holiday homes in these states reflected strong tourism demand, supported by comprehensive transport networks, dynamic economic activities and their proximity to major tourist destinations.
The Holiday Homes Statistics Bulletin: Economic Census 2026 is the first official statistical publication on holiday homes by state and district in Malaysia.
Mohd Uzir said the publication was an important step in strengthening Malaysia's official tourism statistics by providing comprehensive data on the geographical distribution of the industry for the first time.
"These statistics provide an important evidence-based foundation to support policymaking, tourism planning and sustainable industry development," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Full results of Johor election to be known by 10pm, says EC
Article:
KLUANG: The full result of the Johor election, which goes to the polls this Saturday, is expected to be known by 10pm.
Election Commission chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the final outcome could be determined by 10pm following an upgrade to the commission's existing system.
"Let us pray together that with this upgraded system, we can achieve the full results by 10pm.
"We will not be releasing preliminary or partial results; instead, we will announce the complete and final tally," he told reporters after conducting an inspection of the early voting process at Mahkota Camp here today.
Also present was Army chief Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman.
Ramlan urged all eligible voters to turn out in large numbers to vote.
"This is an opportunity for all of us to contribute to the nation through the democratic framework that we practise.
"I hope all voters, regardless of age group, will come forward to cast their ballots on the 11th," he said.
It was reported that some 2.7 million eligible voters are expected to cast their ballots this Saturday.
A total of 56 seats are up for grabs in this election, with early voting taking place today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armed forces reviewing training SOPs after Hobart Camp incidents
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The armed forces are reviewing its training standard operating procedures (SOPs) to strengthen safety measures and facilities while ensuring operational readiness following two explosion incidents at Hobart Camp, Gurun.
Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said the review would focus on identifying areas for improvement in training procedures without compromising the military's combat readiness.
"We are looking at whether there is room to improve theSOPs for conducting training exercises, including the facilities and monitoring at Hobart Camp.
"However, this will not stop us from continuing our training to enhance our combat readiness," he told reporters today after launching the 18th Langkawi International Maritime and Aerospace Exhibition (Lima 2027).
Malek said investigations into the June 16 grenade explosion, which claimed the lives of two soldiers, were now in their final stages, but declined to comment on the findings.
"The investigation is ongoing and is in its final stages. Therefore, I cannot disclose the findings at this time," he said.
He said the armed forces had ensured the victim's family received all assistance and entitlements due to them.
"We have taken care of all matters involving the deceased, including everything the family is entitled to. I also visited the wife of the fallen sergeant in Alor Star on Saturday.
"Although he is no longer with us, he remains part of our armed forces family and we will continue to support them," Malek said.
Yesterday, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the Defence Ministry was awaiting the complete findings of the investigations before deciding on further action.
He also directed the armed forces to reassess its training procedures following two separate explosion incidents at the camp.
Earlier on June 16,
two soldiers were killed
in a grenade explosion during a training exercise at Hobart Camp. Police have since completed their investigation and handed the case to the armed forces for further action.
Less than two weeks later, on June 29, two soldiers suffered leg injuries in another explosion during a live-firing exercise at the same camp.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Service as a lifetime commitment
Article:
LETTER:
I am writing to share a recent personal experience that serves as a powerful reminder of how individuals can continue to inspire and impact their communities long after leaving public office or formal roles.
For several years, many of us watched Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye serve with tireless dedication during his tenure as a Member of Parliament.
What has been truly remarkable, however, is his journey after active politics.
Instead of slipping into a quiet retirement, he transitioned seamlessly into a lifetime of community advocacy, championing public health, safety standards, and social welfare.
He remains a living testament to the fact that an individual's capacity to serve does not expire with a political mandate; his post-political life continues to provide exemplary, yeoman service to the nation.
This spirit of continuous, active citizenship was vividly demonstrated to me just this week.
Late last evening, i made a phone call to Lee and raised a pressing matter, hoping for his action sometime later.
To my utter astonishment, what began as a late-night conversation resulted in direct, decisive action early the next morning.
Lee called Chew Song Kong, the Founder/President  of the Charity Food Basket Society, Klang, who responded immediately to my request. Chew has been doing exceptional work for the less fortunate.
I never expected that a casual discussion would trigger such an immediate and impactful response. Seeing Chew and his organisation move so swiftly to address a community need was deeply moving.
Lee's lifelong dedication and Chew's rapid, heartfelt response show us what is possible when empathy is combined with action.
Their examples have been a profound source of personal inspiration to me, and i am certain they will inspire many others.
We do not need a title or an official platform to make a difference — we simply need the willingness to act when the call comes.
PHILIP MATHEWS
Kuala Lumpur
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IHH Healthcare backs Merdeka 118 tower race to promote preventive health
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IHH Healthcare Malaysia is strengthening its preventive healthcare efforts through a three-year partnership as title partner of The IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118.
Under the partnership, which runs from 2026 to 2028, IHH Healthcare will also serve as the official medical provider for the event, providing medical support through its network of hospitals and healthcare professionals to ensure participant safety throughout the race.
IHH Healthcare Malaysia chief executive officer Dr Kamal Amzan said the company's role in healthcare extends beyond treating patients, focusing also on prevention and healthier communities.
"This race makes health visible, not as a slogan or campaign poster, but as something physical, difficult, shared and, strangely enough, enjoyable," he said at the signing ceremony today.
Beyond The IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118, Kamal said IHH was looking at supporting more initiatives that encourage healthier communities, including collaborations with other major sporting events.
He said IHH was already working with organisers of events such as the Standard Chartered Kuala Lumpur Marathon and Penang Bridge International Marathon to encourage more Malaysians to stay active.
The signing ceremony was witnessed by Deputy Minister of Tourism, Arts and Culture Malaysia (MOTAC), Chiew Choon Man, who said the event would strengthen Malaysia's sports tourism offering ahead of Visit Malaysia 2026 by showcasing the country's landmarks and hospitality.
The IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118 will take place on Aug 22 and 23, with organisers targeting 9,000 participants to climb 118 floors and 2,845 steps.
Fresh Events Asia managing director and race director of the IHH Sky Race | Merdeka 118 Jeffrey Ross said the event had grown from 5,858 participants in its inaugural 2025 edition, which secured two Malaysia Book of Records titles.
"We're going for 9,000 participants as we look to uphold the title of the world's biggest tower climb," he said, adding that participants would make history through a new record attempt.
Ross said the event had attracted participants from more than 70 countries, with around 85 per cent expected to be local and 15 per cent international participants.
He added that organisers were confident of achieving the 9,000-participant target with about 7,500 tickets already sold.
The race is sanctioned by the Malaysian Towerrunning Association and supported by the Malaysia Convention & Exhibition Bureau.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar backs safeguards against arbitrary action on journalists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Action against any journalist or registered media organisation should not be taken arbitrarily, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Instead, he said, any such action should first be referred to the Malaysian Media Council (MMM) before further measures are taken.
Anwar said the mechanism was established to ensure that any action against media practitioners is carried out in a fair, transparent and independent manner.
He said it was also intended to prevent journalists or registered media organisations from being subjected to investigations or prosecution without due consideration.
"Journalists should not be easily subjected to action simply because a complaint has been lodged by any party. The MMM will serve as a preliminary review mechanism before any action is taken.
"Although there is no law anywhere in the world that grants absolute freedom to the media to publish anything it wishes, both the Prime Minister and media practitioners remain bound by the law.
"However, I agree that journalists should not be easily dragged into cases simply because they are perceived to have criticised a government department.
"We established the MMM to facilitate matters for both parties because, if there is any misconduct on the part of the media, it will first be referred to the MMM rather than journalists being automatically investigated or prosecuted," he said during Prime Minister's Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.
He was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Mohd Isam Mohd Isa (BN-Tampin), who raised concerns that several existing laws appeared to restrict press freedom and had affected the country's standing in the World Press Freedom Index.
Anwar added that the government does not take enforcement action against every inaccurate political statement or factual error circulated online.
He said enforcement is generally reserved for cases involving race, religion and royalty (3R), or other serious offences.
Defending the government's approach to regulating online content, Anwar said inaccurate claims and criticism of the government are often addressed through clarification rather than legal action.
He cited inaccurate claims surrounding the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), as well as allegations that he had denied the rights of opposition-led states.
"If there are inaccurate facts, we do not necessarily take action.
"For example, there are figures in Johor claiming that the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone is entirely Johor's absolute right and decision. That is factually wrong. Do we take action? No. Do we report it? No.
"If there are political statements saying the Prime Minister is not intelligent or has denied the rights of opposition states, they are also inaccurate. Do we take action? No. I use the opportunity in Parliament to explain," he said during Ministers' Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.
Anwar said the government's primary concern remained content touching on 3R issues, noting that Malaysia's approach reflected constitutional and institutional responsibilities rather than political considerations.
He said every Conference of Rulers meeting included a dedicated agenda item on 3R cases, with the Inspector-General of Police presenting updates on investigations involving insults against the monarchy or content deemed capable of inciting racial or religious tensions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar wants AIM to provide microcredit financing for youth
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM) has been asked to expand access to microcredit financing for young people.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the microcredit agency would be encouraged to explore ways to improve accessibility for young borrowers through more structured and efficient management and repayment methods.
"I will ask AIM to give special focus to young people.
"The loan amounts may be smaller or moderate, but the management approach and repayment methods can be more structured and efficient," he said.
Anwar was responding to Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman, who proposed that the government establish a microcredit scheme modelled after AIM specifically for those under 35.
Syed Saddiq said the proposed scheme could provide financing between RM10,000 and RM30,000 with a one-off management fee and flexible repayment arrangements similar to AIM.
He noted that existing financing options, including those offered by Tekun Nasional and conventional banks, often involved flat five per cent charges, required businesses to be registered for six months, and involved lengthy documentation processes.
Meanwhile, Anwar clarified that AIM was not exclusively for women, though they formed the majority of its borrowers.
He said data showed that 98 to 99 per cent of women borrowers repaid their loans, while repayment among male borrowers was lower by about two to three per cent.
"If there is a gap among men, we can improve this, and I have increased AIM's loan allocation as announced in the previous Budget," he said.
Anwar said AIM remained an effective microcredit programme due to its lower service rates and large borrower base.
"I agree that we should increase financing opportunities for women and men who need it, because when women are supported, they also help men in the household. But I believe there is a need to increase this," he said.
He added that he had observed low participation among youth during recent engagements in Terengganu, Johor, and Kedah.
"I agree on expanding it to youths. From several meetings in Terengganu, Johor, and Kedah recently, I have not seen many young people utilising AIM financing opportunities," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Data centre jobs can pay up to RM30,000 a month, says Gobind
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's data centre boom is creating high-skilled jobs offering salaries of up to RM30,000 a month, the Dewan Rakyat was told today.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said entry-level positions in the industry offer salaries ranging from RM3,000 to RM7,000, while senior roles can command between RM10,000 and RM30,000 a month.
This is significantly higher than Malaysia's median monthly salary of RM2,793 in 2024.
"Based on the Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) report, From Bytes to Bucks: The Economics of Data Centres in Malaysia, published in the third quarter of 2025, data centre development contributes to the creation of high-skilled jobs such as network engineers, cloud architects and data scientists, with salaries above the national average," he said.
Gobind was responding to a supplementary question from Datuk Muhammad Bakhtiar Wan Chik (PH-Balik Pulau) on the value added by data centre development, including the creation of high-skilled jobs, or whether the industry merely benefited from the country's relatively affordable water and electricity.
He said major technology companies also provide specialised training and certification programmes to develop local talent.
He said the government is also developing a training framework with Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions to produce more skilled workers for the sector.
Meanwhile, Bakhtiar also asked whether the government had assessed the cumulative impact of the rapid expansion of data centres, including AI data centres, on temperatures, energy consumption and water resources, as well as the measures taken to minimise environmental costs for local communities.
Gobind said the government had adopted a forward-looking approach to ensure the expansion of data centres, including AI facilities, is carried out sustainably without causing long-term harm to the environment or local communities.
He said the Data Centre Task Force (DCTF) was established as a national-level coordination and assessment mechanism for all proposed data centre developments.
"Every application for a data centre development is assessed comprehensively, taking into account its cumulative impact on electricity demand, water resources, land use, digital infrastructure and other sustainability factors before approval is considered," he said.
Gobind said operators are encouraged to increase the use of renewable energy, adopt high-efficiency cooling technologies, use alternative water sources and implement rainwater harvesting systems to reduce dependence on treated water.
He said these measures are essential to ensure the growth of the data centre industry does not place disproportionate pressure on the country's energy and water resources.
He added that data centre developments are also subject to the data centre planning guidelines prepared by PLANMalaysia, as well as the planning permission process and reviews by the Department of Environment Malaysia and relevant technical agencies.
"The mechanism ensures factors such as site suitability, utility capacity, environmental risk management and the well-being of local communities are assessed before projects are implemented.
"Developers and data centre operators are encouraged to adopt more sustainable operating practices, including proper electronic waste management, the use of energy-efficient technologies and regular reporting on resource consumption performance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Democrats push key US Senate candidate to quit over sex assault claim
Article:
WASHINGTON: Top Democrats called on scandal-hit US Senate candidate Graham Platner to quit the race on Monday after a sexual assault allegation threatened to derail one of the party's best chances of flipping a Republican-held seat.
Politico reported that Jenny Racicot, a 41-year-old Maine resident who previously dated Platner – whose insurgent rise has drawn comparisons to President Donald Trump – accused him of forcing her to have sex in late 2021.
Platner, a Marine veteran, oysterman and political newcomer who won the state's Democratic primary last month, rejected the allegation.
In a video message posted to X after the report was published, Platner called the allegations "troubling, serious and false."
"Regardless of the inaccuracy of the reporting, but mindful of the political reality it will inflict, we are taking the time to reflect on the best path forward for the state that I love, the people that I love, the movement I belong to, and the goal of defeating Susan Collins," said, referring to the Republican incumbent.
"Those were the goals when we launched this campaign, and they remain my goals today."
The allegation lands at a perilous moment for Democrats, who view Maine as a central battleground in their effort to regain control of the Senate in November's midterm elections.
The Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee (DSCC) said it would pull funding for the Maine Senate race if Platner remains on the ballot.
"The allegations reported today are incredibly disturbing - violence, abuse and sexual assault are absolutely unacceptable," Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer and DSCC Chair Senator Kirsten Gillibrand said in a statement.
"Graham Platner needs to immediately withdraw as the Democratic nominee for Senate and allow Maine Democrats the opportunity to choose a new candidate who can defeat Susan Collins."
Collins, a six-term Republican and one of her party's few remaining moderates in Congress, is among the Democrats' top targets.
The Maine Democratic Party also called on Platner, 41, to withdraw, vowing to "hold every candidate who seeks to represent our state to the highest standard."
Senator Elizabeth Warren, who previously endorsed Platner's bid for the Senate, joined the chorus of Democrats urging him to quit.
"With so much at stake, the best path forward is for Graham Platner to step aside as the Democratic nominee and address these serious allegations outside this Senate race," Warren said in a statement.
Platner's victory in the Democratic primary was hailed by supporters as proof that a blunt anti-establishment message could break through with voters frustrated by cautious, establishment politics.
But his campaign has also alarmed some Democrats who fear his personal baggage could make it harder to defeat Collins in a state where independents and moderate voters often decide elections.
Politico said Racicot alleged that Platner entered her rural Maine home uninvited while deeply intoxicated and forced himself on her.
The latest report follows earlier controversies over old online comments, sexually explicit messages, a tattoo resembling a Nazi symbol that Platner later covered up and allegations that he had mistreated women.
Platner has acknowledged past struggles with undiagnosed PTSD and alcohol abuse, but has denied physically harming former partners.
His supporters argue that his flaws are part of a redemption story and that voters are drawn to his authenticity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MACC: Three arrested in Kedah over alleged RM20m financing bid for paddy and rice purchases that never happened
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Three people, including two company directors, have been arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in Kedah over allegations they used false documents in an attempt to obtain about RM20 million in financing for paddy and rice purchases that investigators say never took place.
In a statement today, the anti-graft agency said the suspects, who are involved in the rice and paddy production and processing business, are suspected of submitting documents containing false particulars to a development financial institution under the Trade Working Capital Financing-I facility.
The financing, worth about RM20 million, was intended for the purchase of paddy and rice.
According to the MACC statement, one of the suspects, a man in his 30s who works as an operations manager, has been remanded for five days until July 11 after an order was granted by Magistrate Eliana Ismail at the Alor Setar Civil Court this morning.
The suspect was arrested at about 6pm yesterday when he turned up to give his statement at the MACC office in Alor Setar.
Preliminary investigations indicate that the three suspects, who are members of the same family, allegedly conspired to commit the offence in 2025.
“The other two male suspects, who are company directors in their 60s, have been released on MACC bail,” the statement said.
The commission alleged that although the suspects submitted the documents to the development financial institution, no actual transaction for the purchase of paddy and rice took place.
MACC Senior Director of Investigations Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar confirmed the arrests when contacted and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.

Title: In Larkin, land leases and a city’s growing pains shape Johor election contest
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Land lease renewals in Kampung Melayu Majidee and the need to modernise public infrastructure have emerged as the key issues shaping the contest for the Larkin state seat in the 16th Johor state election.
The contest pits incumbent Mohd Hairi Mad Shah of Barisan Nasional (BN) against Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Suhaizan Kaiat, with both candidates offering contrasting approaches to balancing development with the preservation of the long-established community.
Mohd Hairi, who is also State Youth, Sports, Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Committee chairman, said the state government remained committed to safeguarding Kampung Melayu Majidee through lease renewal offers of between 60 and 99 years, either on an individual or lot basis, as well as a 50 per cent premium discount to ease the financial burden on residents.
“The move clearly demonstrates the state government’s commitment to preserving the village for future generations, ensuring it remains well-managed while sustaining the Malay community’s presence in the heart of the city,” he told Bernama.
The one-term incumbent said the land issue should not be politicised, but instead resolved based on facts.
Suhaizan, who is Pulai MP, however, said the state government’s measures had yet to meet residents’ expectations.
He proposed a ‘dual-track’ approach involving parallel negotiations between the government and the local community to resolve the long-running issue, noting that residents were seeking permanent land ownership instead of lease extensions.
Beyond the land issue, both candidates agreed that ensuring Larkin remained sustainable and relevant as Johor Bahru continued to develop was another key challenge.
Mohd Hairi acknowledged the need to improve public amenities, particularly the shortage of parking spaces, which he said had been worsened by cross-border workers leaving their vehicles near the Larkin Sentral Terminal.
He said he was confident the Johor Public Transport Corporation (PAJ) would implement a comprehensive solution to address the issue if BN was returned to power.
On physical development, Mohd Hairi also highlighted his track record, citing his role in securing two of Johor’s four Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor (SRBJ) initiative for Larkin.
“I also succeeded in relocating squatters living along the railway tracks in Larkin, who were frequently affected by floods, to more comfortable flat units,” he said.
Suhaizan, meanwhile, said greater attention should be given to expanding affordable home ownership opportunities for residents of the People’s Housing Project (PPR), particularly to address household overcrowding as well as management and maintenance issues at low-cost housing schemes.
“I am impressed by the approach adopted by the Pasir Gudang City Council, where problematic flats are taken over for maintenance, management corporations (MCs) are trained and assisted with maintenance issues before the properties are handed back once they are back on track,” he said, adding that a similar model could be implemented in Larkin if he was given the mandate.
Also contesting the Larkin seat is Bersama candidate Norsinah Abu.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 state seats in the state election, with more than 2.7 million registered voters set to cast their ballots on July 11.
For the latest updates on the election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: ‘The tears just won’t stop’: Father says Banting stabbing victim leaves ICU but remains traumatised
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — The 15-year-old girl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting remains deeply traumatised despite her condition stabilising after surgery, her father said today.
As she begins what is expected to be a week-long recovery in hospital, he said “the tears just won’t stop”.
Speaking to
Sinar Harian
at Banting Hospital, Mohd Firuz Ibrahim said his daughter had been moved out of the intensive care unit (ICU) after surgery and was now under close observation as she continued her recovery.
“My daughter is still traumatised. The tears just won’t stop flowing,” he said.
Mohd Firuz said doctors and nurses were continuing to monitor the teenager’s condition.
“This morning, thank God, she looks more stable, and she told me she doesn’t feel too much pain anymore.
“The tube will remain in her lung for another two or three days. The doctors will remove it once they are satisfied the bleeding has stopped.
“She was admitted to the ICU yesterday and transferred to a general ward last night after spending four hours in surgery,” he said, according to the national daily.
He added that his daughter had started talking again and was able to drink and eat light meals.
Asked when she might be discharged, Mohd Firuz said doctors expected she would remain in hospital for at least a week, depending on her recovery.
“As a father, it is heartbreaking to see your child like this.
“We have never even hit her. Even a small cut is painful, let alone being stabbed. What’s happened has happened, and we accept it with patience and faith,” he said.
The stabbing occurred at about 9.30am yesterday while the victim was at the school’s canteen.
Police said the 15-year-old suspect, who is also a student at the school, allegedly entered the school through the main entrance before stabbing the victim multiple times.
The victim was rushed to Banting Hospital, while the suspect was arrested shortly after the incident.
Kuala Langat district police chief Superintendent Mohd Akmalrizal Radzi said the 15-year-old suspect has been remanded for four days until Friday to assist investigations under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder.

Title: Perak Ruler officiates Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta
Article:
IPOH, July 7 — The Sultan of Perak, Sultan Nazrin Shah, today officially opened the Sultan Nazrin Shah 69 Commando Camp in Ulu Kinta here.
The Raja Permaisuri of Perak, Tuanku Zara Salim, the Raja Muda of Perak, Raja Jaafar Raja Muda Musa, and the Raja DiHilir of Perak, Raja Iskandar Dzurkarnain Sultan Idris Shah also attended the event.
Also present were Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail.
In his speech, Saifuddin Nasution said the opening of the camp symbolises the MADANI Government’s commitment to strengthening the capabilities of the nation’s security forces to address increasingly complex and evolving security challenges.
“This camp reflects the government’s commitment to providing the best facilities, infrastructure and training environment to ensure the force remains fully prepared to safeguard the nation’s peace and sovereignty,” he said.
Saifuddin Nasution said Malaysia was ranked 12th out of 163 countries in the latest Global Peace Index (GPI), an achievement that is a source of pride for the country’s security forces.
He said the contributions of the 69 Commando unit, established in 1969, have been proven through its involvement in numerous key operations, including combating the communist insurgency, the Al-Maunah incident in 2000, Ops Daulat in Lahad Datu, Sabah, in 2013, and the country’s special deployment in connection with the MH17 tragedy in 2014.
“In addition, the unit has played a key role in counter-terrorism operations, hostage rescue missions and responding to high-risk security threats.
“Whenever the nation has faced threats to its sovereignty and security, the 69 Commando has always been at the forefront,” he said.
In recognition of the unit’s service and sacrifices, he said the government developed the Sultan Nazrin Shah Camp as the new main base for the 69 Commando.
Saifuddin Nasution said the camp was built at a cost of more than RM206 million on a 338-hectare site. It was meticulously planned to meet operational requirements before being handed over to the Royal Malaysia Police in 2024 as a modern and comprehensive operations, training and administrative complex.
“The Sultan Nazrin Shah Camp is a strategic national investment that will serve as a catalyst for enhancing the 69 Commando’s capabilities for decades to come.
“This camp is not merely a physical development, but also a symbol of the nation’s appreciation for the service and sacrifices of generations of 69 Commando officers and personnel who have served for more than five decades,” he said.
He said the camp would nurture a new generation of 69 Commando personnel to carry on the unit’s legacy, while serving as a centre to strengthen expertise, fighting spirit and strategic cooperation to ensure Malaysia remains peaceful, stable and sovereign.
He expressed confidence that the Sultan Nazrin Shah Camp would continue to stand as a symbol of the 69 Commando’s excellence, courage and sacrifice in defending the nation’s peace, security and sovereignty. — Bernama

Title: Election Commission says 588 complaints lodged as Johor polls enter final stretch
Article:
KLUANG, July 7 — The Election Commission (EC) has received 588 complaints involving various election offences during the campaign period for the 16th Johor State Election, as of today.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said that, of the total, 44 police reports had been lodged.
“In addition, three reports have also been lodged with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) over related offences,” he told a press conference at Dewan Mahkota, Kem Mahkota, here today.
Earlier, Ramlan, accompanied by Army Chief General Tan Sri Azhan Md Othman, monitored the smooth conduct of the polling process at several early voting centres, including the Kluang District Police Headquarters and Kem Mahkota.
The early voting process involved 20,607 people, comprising 8,544 members of the Malaysian Armed Forces and their spouses as well as 12,063 police personnel and their spouses.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 seats in the Johor state election, with polling set for this Saturday. — Bernama

Title: Environment Department denies issuing viral 2024 state cleanliness infographic
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, July 7 — The Department of Environment (DOE) has denied issuing, publishing or verifying the infographic titled “
Ranking Kebersihan Negeri Malaysia 2024
”, which has been widely circulated on social media platforms and messaging applications.
The DOE said in a statement today that it had never released any media statement, report or response regarding the purported ranking of states as displayed in the infographic.
